hypothesis: A hybrid adaptive batching algorithm that dynamically switches between static-64 and dynamic batching strategies based on a real-time estimate of the current graph-size variance in the training buffer will achieve greater training speedup than either algorithm alone across datasets with non-stationary or curriculum-ordered graph-size distributions, because it minimizes recompilation overhead during early training while exploiting the lower per-step memory footprint of dynamic batching once the compilation cache stabilizes.
